message:
fix(landing): align operator preview symbols with Operator Framework section

The "Operator preview" in the Research Lab CTA section was using different
symbols (Σ, ⊘, ⟳, ⊙) than the Operator Framework section (⊘, ✂, ⟂, ⊞).

This caused confusion as users would see two different symbol sets for
the same four operators on the same page.

Now uses consistent symbols per the f8vs.6 spec:
- ⊘ Level-Split
- ✂ Exclusion-Test
- ⟂ Object-Transpose
- ⊞ Scale-Check

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -710,10 +710,10 @@ export default function Home() { {/* Operator preview */}
{[ - { symbol: "Σ", name: "Level Split" }, - { symbol: "⊘", name: "Exclusion Test" }, - { symbol: "⟳", name: "Object Transpose" }, - { symbol: "⊙", name: "Scale Check" }, + { symbol: "⊘", name: "Level-Split" }, + { symbol: "✂", name: "Exclusion-Test" }, + { symbol: "⟂", name: "Object-Transpose" }, + { symbol: "⊞", name: "Scale-Check" }, ].map((op) => (
{op.symbol} @@ -1015,10 +1015,10 @@ brenner session compile --thread-id RS-20260105`}
- Selection bias detected — how will you ensure random sampling? + Selection bias detected - how will you ensure random sampling?
- Reverse causation possible — can you establish temporal order? + Reverse causation possible - can you establish temporal order?
@@ -1145,7 +1145,7 @@ brenner session compile --thread-id RS-20260105`} Offline

{similarityMatches.map((match) => { @@ -1165,7 +1165,7 @@ brenner session compile --thread-id RS-20260105`} })}

- Runs entirely client-side — your hypotheses never leave your machine. + Runs entirely client-side - your hypotheses never leave your machine.
